data.description: A blast of colored light and sound flashes in a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on a point you can see within range. Each creature in that area must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be charmed until the end of your next turn. A charmed target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success.
An instantaneous spell harms, heals, creates, or alters a place, object, or creature instantaneously.

You create a scroll or other suitable paper and imbue it with potent materialize that lasts the duration. To you and creatures you designate, any writing on the scrol appears normal, but when any other creature reads the writing, it appears as an illusion conveying the meaning intended by you. If the writing was in an unknown magical language, it is unintelligible to everyone. Alternatively, you can cause the message, or entire writing, to appear to be written in a different language, and that language must be one you know. Should the spell be dispelled, both the illusion and the original scroll disappear.
